channel.title: CyberNetSec - Patch Management
channel.description: Latest patch management threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: Ivanti Patches Critical Sentry Flaws Allowing Root-Level RCE
item.description:
Ivanti has released urgent security updates for two critical vulnerabilities in its Ivanti Sentry (formerly MobileIron Sentry) product. The most severe flaw, CVE-2026-10520, is an unauthenticated OS command injection vulnerability that allows a remote attacker to achieve root-level remote code execu...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Mobile Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Mandates Risk-Based Patching for Federal Agencies with New Directive BOD 26-04
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has issued Binding Operational Directive (BOD) 26-04, fundamentally changing how federal civilian agencies must manage cybersecurity vulnerabilities. The new directive, effective June 10, 2026, mandates a risk-based prioritization mode...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Microsoft's Record-Breaking June Patch Tuesday: Over 200 Flaws and Three Zero-Days Patched
item.description:
Microsoft has released its largest-ever Patch Tuesday for June 2026, addressing a staggering 200 vulnerabilities across its product ecosystem. The update includes fixes for 33 critical flaws and three publicly disclosed zero-day vulnerabilities. Among the most severe are a wormable Windows Kernel RC...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Patch Management, Vulnerability, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: ICS Patch Tuesday: Siemens, Schneider, Phoenix Contact Release Critical Advisories
item.description:
The June 2026 Industrial Control Systems (ICS) Patch Tuesday featured important security advisories from major OT vendors including Siemens, Schneider Electric, and Phoenix Contact. Siemens released four advisories covering multiple flaws in Sinec INS, Siprotec 5 relays, and other products, includin...

Severity: High

Categories: Industrial Control Systems, Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Active Exploitation of Critical PAN-OS Auth Bypass (CVE-2026-0257) Detected in the Wild
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has issued a threat brief detailing the active exploitation of CVE-2026-0257, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability affecting PAN-OS software. Unidentified attackers are exploiting the flaw to gain unauthorized access to GlobalProtect VPN services. This vulnerabi...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Google Patches Fifth Actively Exploited Chrome Zero-Day of 2026
item.description:
Google has released an emergency security update for its Chrome browser to patch CVE-2026-11645, a high-severity zero-day vulnerability in the V8 JavaScript engine. The flaw, which allows for arbitrary code execution, is the fifth actively exploited Chrome vulnerability addressed this year. Users ar...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA Mandates Patch for Actively Exploited SolarWinds DoS Flaw Added to KEV Catalog
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a critical denial-of-service (DoS) vulnerability in SolarWinds Serv-U file transfer software, CVE-2026-28318, to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. This action confirms the vulnerability is under active attack...

Severity: High

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: CVSS 10.0 Flaws in Azure HorizonDB and DbGate Expose Cloud Environments to RCE
item.description:
A weekly threat intelligence report has disclosed two separate, critical vulnerabilities with a maximum CVSS severity score of 10.0, affecting Azure HorizonDB and DbGate. The first, CVE-2026-48567, is an unauthenticated privilege bypass in Azure HorizonDB that allows a network attacker to gain eleva...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cloud Security, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA KEV Alert: Two-Year-Old Oracle WebLogic Flaw Now Under Active Attack
item.description:
A two-year-old, high-severity vulnerability in Oracle WebLogic Server, CVE-2024-21182, is being actively exploited in the wild, prompting CISA to add it to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog. The flaw, originally patched in July 2024, allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to compr...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Actively Exploited Zero-Day in Cisco SD-WAN Allows Root Access, No Patch Available
item.description:
Cisco has issued an urgent warning for a high-severity zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2026-20245, affecting its Catalyst SD-WAN products. The flaw is being actively exploited in the wild, allowing unauthenticated attackers to execute commands with root privileges. With a CVSS score of 7.8, the vulnerab...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →
